title: Aggregate Functions
content:
### Aggregate Functions

Aggregate functions perform calculations on a set of rows and return a single value. They are commonly used with GROUP BY.

---

### Common Aggregate Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| COUNT() | Returns the number of rows |
| SUM() | Returns the sum of values |
| AVG() | Returns the average value |
| MAX() | Returns the maximum value |
| MIN() | Returns the minimum value |

### COUNT Examples

```sql
-- Count all rows
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees;

-- Count non-null values in a column
SELECT COUNT(salary) FROM employees;

-- Count distinct values
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT department) FROM employees;
```

### SUM and AVG Examples

```sql
SELECT SUM(salary) AS total_payroll,
       AVG(salary) AS average_salary,
       ROUND(AVG(salary), 2) AS rounded_avg
FROM employees;
```

### MAX and MIN Examples

```sql
SELECT MAX(salary) AS highest_salary,
       MIN(salary) AS lowest_salary,
       MAX(hire_date) AS most_recent_hire,
       MIN(hire_date) AS earliest_hire
FROM employees;
```

### NULL Handling

| Function | NULL Behavior |
|----------|---------------|
| COUNT(*) | Counts all rows regardless of NULLs |
| COUNT(col) | Ignores NULL values |
| SUM(col) | Ignores NULL values |
| AVG(col) | Ignores NULL values |
| MAX/MIN(col) | Ignores NULL values |

> **Key Point:** Aggregate functions except COUNT(*) ignore NULL values. COUNT(*) counts all rows, while COUNT(column) counts only non-null values.
codeExample:
SELECT department,
       COUNT(*) AS employee_count,
       AVG(salary) AS avg_salary,
       MAX(salary) AS max_salary
FROM employees
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;

quiz.question: Which aggregate function returns the number of rows?
How does AVG() handle NULL values?
